what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger entry that summarizes and annotates the claude-ds analysis of landing-mockups.md, capturing observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, themes, and embedding summaries for a web-surface stress test of a generative design model.
observations: The file is a JSON interpretive record for the claude-ds analysis located at analysis/landing-mockups.md.
It documents a web-surface stress test of three tall fixed-width (1223px) landing pages from ingest/landing-mockups/.
It maps deck primitives to web equivalents: Slide -> section band, Sequence -> scroll, Zoning -> alternating band backgrounds plus a dark footer, Atoms -> 1:1 transfer.

It lists additive web-chrome atoms: persistent top nav, segmented audience toggle, hero band with scroll cue, content-LOD placeholder boxes, pricing comparison table, repeated CTA bands, and dark footer.
It extends AXES.md by adding a surface axis spanning paged (slide) to scrolling (web) and generalizing Slide to band/frame.
It unifies audience-variant across variant slides, variant pages, and a live toggle as one {template + audience param} mechanism.
It reads grey placeholder boxes as evidence of a template-with-typed-slots content-LOD model rather than unfinished artwork.
It uses an evidence-diff discipline marking findings as confirms, extends (pencil), new (plus), or contradicts (warning).
It asserts 'No contradictions' and claims the web surface needed zero new grammar.
It declares cross-folder confirmation across four folders but does not include the per-folder evidence here.
The symbols array is empty because the file contains no code symbols.
standouts: Reframing scroll as the same containment tree traversed spatially rather than temporally: slide becomes band, sequence becomes scroll.
Unifying audience-variant across slides, pages, and a live segmented toggle under a single {template + audience param} mechanism.
Treating wireframe placeholder boxes as intentional typed-slot LOD evidence instead of missing assets.
Extending AXES.md with a generative surface axis from paged to scrolling.
conventions: JSON ledger schema with project, path, model, what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, summary_for_embedding, intention_for_embedding, and symbols.
Evidence-diff discipline: confirms / extends / new / contradicts.
axis=f(content,position): surface treated as a generative axis, not a separate design language.
Single-mechanism reuse: one audience-variant mechanism spans multiple surfaces.
concerns: The verdict of 'no new grammar' rests on three fixed-width desktop mockups; mobile reflow is inferred, not observed.
Cross-folder confidence claims ('Confirmed across 4 folders') are summary assertions whose per-folder evidence lives in other analysis docs.
The reading of grey placeholder boxes as intentional typed-slot LOD may be interpretation rather than source author intent.
The symbols array is empty, so this ledger entry carries no code-symbol anchors.
notes: Interpretive ledger entry that reads three fixed-width landing mockups as proof the deck grammar survives intact on a scrolling web surface, logging only additive web-chrome atoms.
questions: Is mobile reflow at narrow viewports verified anywhere, or only predicted from the deck's responsive rules?
Are the grey placeholder boxes an intentional authored LOD state in the source, or an analyst's interpretation of unfinished mockups?
Where does the per-folder evidence for the 'Confirmed across 4 folders' claim live?
Is the surface-axis extension already present in AXES.md, or is it being proposed in this file?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it is framed as the web-surface stress test and records the mapping, verdict, and additive atoms for that test.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to prove the generative design model is surface-agnostic so that decks and web pages can be emitted from one source, and to keep the ledger focused on only the genuinely new web atoms rather than duplicating grammar.
novelty: Frames a scrolling web landing page as a re-rendering of a paged deck's top level (slide->band, sequence->scroll) and treats wireframe placeholder boxes as proof of a typed-slot LOD model.
contribution: Contributes a surface-axis extension (paged<->scrolling, Slide generalized to band/frame), a unified audience-variant mechanism across slides/pages/toggle, a logged set of additive web-chrome atoms, and an interpretive reading of placeholder boxes as typed-slot LOD evidence.
summary_for_embedding: JSON interpretive ledger entry for the claude-ds analysis of landing-mockups.md, recording a web-surface stress test that maps deck containment tree, zoning, atoms, and narrative arc to a scrolling fixed-width landing page with only additive web-chrome atoms.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to prove the generative design model is surface-agnostic and to unify audience-variant and surface-axis concepts across paged decks and scrolling web pages.
